channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Mina the Hollower sells 300,000 copies in three days
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

June 3, 2026

1 Min Read

Image via Yacht Club Games

Mina the Hollower has sold 300,000 units worldwide in three days, according to developer Yacht Club Games.

Studio co-founder Sean Velasco told Bloomberg the milestone means Yacht Club will be able to continue operating without the need for layoffs or outside investment. 

Mina the Hollower launched on May 29, 2026, on PC and console platforms including the Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation 5, Steam, and Xbox Series X | S.

The acclaimed action-adventure game was in development for over six years and was delayed towards the end of 2025 in order to add polish. Around that time, Velasco told Bloomberg that Yacht Club needed Mina the Hollower to deliver the goods in order to ensure the 15-person studio's survival.

"It's make-or-break for sure," he said in December. "If we sold 500,000 copies, then we would be golden. If we sold even 200,000, that would be really, really great. If we sold, like, 100,000, that’s not so good."

Related:Tekken 8 game director Kohei Ikeda has left Bandai Namco

Prior to launch, Yacht Club had burned through its capital and placed a second project—a 3D version of Shovel Knight—on hold to focus on Mina the Hollower. It also laid off a number of employees after making the title its primary focus.

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton.
